title: spergula arvensis var. pomeliana
intro: Spergula arvensis var. pomeliana, also known as a variant of the Corn Spurry, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Caryophyllaceae family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include being a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as kidney stones, urinary tract infections, and skin issues. The health benefits of Spergula arvensis var. pomeliana include its ability to reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Traditionally, the herbal preparations of Spergula arvensis var. pomeliana include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
